response:
Short answer: fazure-mcp sits behind App Gateway/WAF and APIM and uses the APIM "tri‑auth" pattern:

Flow (high level)
- Client -> Azure Application Gateway (WAF) -> APIM (API Management) -> fazure‑mcp backend.
- The App Gateway/WAF enforces network protections and site‑specific rules (e.g. FazureMcpVpnOnly, trusted IP allowlists for Rackspace VPN / Janus AWS).
- APIM enforces authentication and authorization before forwarding requests to the backend.

APIM "tri‑auth" checks (all three are required)
1. DDI tenant isolation
   - Implemented via the x-auth-ddi header and APIM policy XML (rewrite/preservation logic).
   - APIM enforces tenant context and forwards X-DDI to the backend.
   - This is the most common failure point when tenant context is lost (e.g. in retry loops).

2. Service‑to‑service token exchange (Azure AD)
   - Requests are validated via an Azure AD app registration / token exchange for service identity.

3. Subscription key
   - Subscription key is used for rate limiting / usage control.

Other APIM behavior relevant to fazure‑mcp
- APIM also validates Rackspace X‑Auth‑Token (classification into racker vs customer), maps role groups, enforces route/category RBAC, and forwards trusted headers to the backend (X-User-Type, X-Auth-Method, X-Access-Level, X-User-Roles, X-DDI, etc.).

Debug notes
- When troubleshooting auth failures for fazure‑mcp, check DDI (x-auth-ddi) first, then AAD token validity, then subscription key. Also review WAF logs: per‑listener WAF policies may inspect bodies and generate false positives (causing 403s).

AWS IPs, added the full Janus AWS address set 52.39.2.74, 52.41.136.221, 52.10.115.132, and 52.40.141.99 to the trusted IP allowlists for fazure MCP and oauth endpoints on Azure Application Gateway. Root cause was that recent POST /mcp failures on fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com at 2026-04-14T17:51:41Z through 2026-04-14T18:06:13Z were blocked by custom rule VpnOnly under policy scope fazure-mcp, even though those IPs are the known JanusAWS set alrea", "2026-04-15: On 2026-04-15, investigation of fazure-mcp 500/502/403 traffic found that the current issue was not WAF regression and not backend 500s. In AGWAccessLogs for fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com over the prior 24h, there were 0 HTTP 500 responses, multiple 502s concentrated around 13:46-13:50 UTC and 14:14-14:15 UTC and especially 14:38-14:51 UTC, and a small number of 403s. AGWFirewallLogs showed trusted Rackspace/Janus traffic matching AllowTrustedVpnFazureMcp and current 403s were mainly expected", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated fazure-mcp WAF 403s for POST /mcp from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181. Local Azure-MCP rollout artifacts showed the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites was created by copying managedRules and policySettings from the global policy, then attaching only site custom rules RateLimit, VpnOnly, BlockSensitiveFiles, BlockKnownPath, GeoBlocking, and AllowSafeFilters. The global RackspaceVPN Allow rule was not copied into the site ", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, a reusable WAF mitigation pattern was established for trusted internal MCP SQL traffic behind Azure Application Gateway. Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.181 was receiving 403s on POST /mcp to fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com because the per-listener WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax-fazure-mcp-sites allowed the VPN through the VpnOnly rule but still sent the JSON request body through OWASP managed inspection. AGWFirewallLogs showed SQL injection false positives on AR", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated 403s from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.178 to fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/register. Live AGW logs showed OPTIONS /register returned 200, while POST /register was blocked by global WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax with rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) on client_uri=https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector and redirect_uris=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug, followed by 949110 anomaly sco", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19 in /Users/benj8956/Downloads/Azure-MCP, updated scripts/fazure_lockdown.sh and applied the expanded App Gateway/WAF lockdown for appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax. The shared RateLimit rule at priority 1 and FazureMcpVpnOnly rule at priority 7 now cover four MCP hosts: fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com, fazure-mcp-dev.mscloud.rackspace.com, fazure-cosmos-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com, and fazure-cosmos-mcp-dev.mscloud.rackspace.com. OAuth remains scoped separately under FazureOauthV", "2026-03-19: On 2026-03-19, live investigation of App Gateway appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax found two independent exposure paths for fazure-mcp/fazure-oauth. First, the WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax is in Prevention mode but only has Allow rules for RackspaceVPN and JanusAWS; it has no catch-all Block for non-allowed IPs. From external client IP 47.221.164.73, requests to fazure-mcp.mscloud.rackspace.com/health returned 200 and /mcp returned 401 through the gateway, and AGWFire", "2026-03-17: Resolved the MFA Phase 2 ARM template deployment issue. The problem was that the ARM template specified resource scopes that didn't align with the new MFA policy requirements. When MFA was enforced, the service principal couldn't authenticate because the ARM scope was set to subscription-level but the MFA policy required resource-group-level scope. Fix: Updated ARM scope definitions to use resource-group-level scoping. Used az rest instead of curl for the API calls because az rest handles token ", "2026-03-19: In azr-compliance-v3, X-Auth-Token authorization is defined in APIM, not OpenAPI. APIM validates the Rackspace token, classifies the caller as racker or customer, maps racker named-value groups to MSP-Admin/MSP-Engineer/MSP-Viewer, maps customer roles to admin/billing/observer, enforces route/category RBAC in api-policy.xml, then forwards trusted headers like X-User-Type, X-Auth-Method, X-Access-Level, X-User-Roles, and X-DDI to the backend. The actual racker group display-name values are enviro", "2026-03-17: APIM uses a tri-auth pattern for service authentication: (1) DDI tenant isolation via x-auth-ddi header, (2) service-to-service token exchange via Azure AD app registration, and (3) subscription key for rate limiting. All three must pass for a request to be authorized. When debugging auth issues, always check DDI isolation first — it's the most common failure point because tenant context gets lost in retry loops."]}
